mendations will be the cutting off of
the electric street sprinkler run over
the tracks of the Washington Water
Power company.


This sprinkler has been tn, use
of more complaint from citizens than
any other part of the street system
Of sprinkling.


The city pays to the Washington
Water Power company $3.50 a month
from May l to October for the
sprinkler, regardless of whether it is
run or left In the barns. or whether
1t rains or the sun shines.


This money goes to the corporation
and the work done is so unsatisfac-
tory that the board OF public works
last month seriously considered the
plan to abrogate the contract.


Four steam sprinklers can be put
on for $500 a month and the work
done is much more satisfactory than
the careless surface work of the
corporation sprinkler.


Because of this the board OF public
works will recommend the cancella-
tion of the contract with the WASH
ington Water Power company to pre-
tend to sprinkle the streets.
